title: Waltham police searching for girl, 17, missing since July 3
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Victoria Amore, 17, went missing from her Waltham home on July 3, last seen entering a dark sedan.
Amore suffers from severe food allergies, asthma, and seizures, and did not take her EpiPen or inhaler.
Waltham police urge anyone with information about Amore's location to contact them at 781-314-3600.

title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old female bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth on Monday.
The truck driver remained at the scene and was cooperative; no charges or citations have been announced.
Massachusetts State Police and Dartmouth Police are investigating the crash, which follows other recent fatal incidents in the area.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, faces charges for assaulting a Burger King employee and a customer on June 30 in East Boston.
Donovan allegedly used racial slurs and resisted arrest at MBTA Maverick Station before being taken into custody.
Donovan pleaded not guilty at arraignment and was released, ordered to stay away from Burger King and Maverick Square.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, from Canton, charged with murder and firearm offenses in the July 4 killing of Xavier Bautista.
Xavier Bautista, a Cambridge DPW worker, was shot and killed by masked assailants on July 4 near Broadway and Norfolk Street.
The incident has reignited debate over the effectiveness of gunshot detection technology in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died after a skydiving accident at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts on Sunday.
Authorities report the man fell 30 feet due to a gust of wind interfering with his parachute.
This incident marks the second fatality at Jumptown Skydiving in the past year, after a previous accident in October.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's request for postpartum mental illness testimony from other women was denied ahead of her murder trial.
Judge William Sullivan plans to select 18 jurors for Clancy's trial, starting jury selection on July 20.
Clancy, facing charges of murdering her three children, plans to argue an insanity defense due to postpartum issues.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranked No. 8 among NFL quarterbacks by ESPN after finishing second in MVP voting.
Maye led the NFL in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage last season.
The Patriots improved to a 14-3 record and a Super Bowl appearance under Maye's leadership.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship in Seabrook, N.H., eating 7.77 pounds of wings.
Webb narrowly defeated 18-time Nathan’s Hot Dog winner Joey Chestnut, who ate 7.54 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Massachusetts native Geoff Esper, known for his Hooters wing record, finished third in the competition.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots linebackers, coached by Mike Vrabel, were a top unit in 2025, ranking third by Pro Football Focus.
Key players include Harold Landry, who led in sacks, and Robert Spillane, who led in tackles last season.
Rookie Gabe Jacas remains unsigned, while Elijah Ponder is expected to develop further after a promising 2025 season.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Man dies after apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph, on Sunday afternoon, per Norfolk District Attorney’s Office.
Randolph police and fire received 911 call about distressed swimmer at Great Pond around 4 p.m.
State Police are investigating the incident; further information unavailable as of Monday afternoon.
